title: Agent Response Envelope (ARE)
description: The ARE is the standard task and communication envelope for the Claude Family of Agents framework. It defines what a task is, who should do it, what done looks like, and how results travel back. It is runner-agnostic and transport-agnostic. Runner adapters (LangGraph, CrewAI, etc.) translate the envelope into runner-native constructs. Transport is handled by A2A.

sap_id.description: The SAP ID that originated this envelope. Carries forward from the inbound message for full traceability from raw signal to agent output.

authorization_level.description: Authorization requirement for this specific envelope step. Overrides task.priority-driven gate behavior for this step only. none: no gate call, execute immediately. standard: gate call, urgency drives whether it blocks. elevated: gate call, always blocks regardless of urgency. explicit: gate call, blocks AND requires a named approver defined in review.reviewer.

routing_finalization.description: Jean-Claude final assignment record for non-critical work-agent dispatches. Captures whether deterministic routing was accepted or overridden after Joan-clode scoring review.

finalized.description: Whether final assignment has been explicitly confirmed.

finalized_by.description: Singleton that finalized the assignment. Must be Jean-Claude for standard routing flow.

accepted_deterministic.description: True when Jean-Claude accepted the deterministic recommendation; false when overridden.

scoring_confidence.description: Confidence value carried from SAP scoring output.

rationale.description: Short finalization note. Required when accepted_deterministic is false.

envelope_id.description: Unique identifier for this envelope. Format: ARE-{YYYYMMDD}-{sequence}. Immutable once created.

schema_version.description: Version of the ARE schema this envelope conforms to.

parent_envelope_id.description: ARE ID of the parent envelope if this is a sub-task. Null for top-level tasks.

session_id.description: Optional session identifier for grouping related envelopes across a work session.

from.description: The sender of this envelope. Always Jean-Claude for human-initiated tasks. May be another agent for sub-tasks if sub-routing is granted.

agent_id.description: Full Vibe-Responsibility name of the sending agent, or a singleton name.

role.description: Whether the sender is a human, a singleton (Clyde / Jean-Claude / Joan Claude), or a standard Vibe-Responsibility agent.

runner.description: The runner the sending agent is operating on. Null if unknown or not applicable.

to.description: One or more receivers. Jean-Claude may route to multiple agents in parallel, including the same responsibility with different vibes.

agent_id.description: Target receiver name. Prefer Vibe-Responsibility format for composed work agents. Singleton and infrastructure receiver names are also valid.

execution.description: SPAWN: create a fresh agent instance with full context. HANDOFF: pass to an already-running instance (performance optimization only — envelope must still be complete enough to spawn if needed).

runner_hint.description: Optional hint to the adapter layer about which runner to use. Null means let the adapter decide via agent card discovery.

endpoint.description: A2A agent card URL or local identifier. Null for local same-runner agents.

allow_sub_routing.description: Whether this agent is permitted to spawn or route to additional agents to complete this task. Default false. Jean-Claude sets this explicitly.

parallel.description: Whether this receiver runs in parallel with other receivers in the to[] array. If false, receivers execute sequentially in array order.

task.description: What the agent is being asked to do.

title.description: Short human-readable task name. Used in logs, git commits, and status reports.
examples: Diagnose vacuum drop on Line C

description.description: Full task description. Plain language. The agent reads this as its primary instruction.

intent.description: The category of work being requested. Used by the adapter layer and Jean-Claude for routing logic.

priority.description: Task urgency. Critical tasks may preempt running tasks depending on runner implementation.

constraints.description: Explicit constraints the agent must respect while completing the task.
examples: Do not modify any files outside the SapSpyBuddy directory
Plain English output only — no technical jargon
Maximum 6 action items in the output

spawn.description: Everything needed to instantiate the agent fresh. The runner adapter uses this to construct the agent. Must be complete enough to spawn even if execution=handoff.

vibe.description: The vibe name from the registry. Seeds the agent's personality and approach.

responsibility.description: The responsibility name. Defines the job.

vibe_constitution.description: Optional explicit personality definition for this vibe. If null, the runner looks up the vibe in the registry. Inline here overrides the registry.
examples: Diagnostic, tenacious, finds what's hidden. Never accepts the first explanation. Looks for the pattern underneath the pattern.

context.description: The context window package for this agent. Everything it needs, nothing it doesn't.

files.description: File references the agent should load. Resolved by the runner at spawn time.

repo.description: Repo name if not the current repo.

required.description: If true, spawn fails if this file cannot be loaded.

inline.description: Short inline context string. Use for anything under ~200 words. Longer content must be a file reference.

exclude.description: Explicit exclusion list. Paths or patterns the agent must not load, even if they would otherwise be in scope.

tools.description: MCP tool names this agent is permitted to use. Empty array means no tool access. Omitting this field grants default tool access per runner config.

done.description: What done looks like. The agent reads this to understand the finish line. The reviewer uses this to assess the output.

acceptance_criteria.description: Explicit list of conditions that must be true for the task to be considered complete. Written from the reviewer's perspective.
examples: All zones with flags have a ranked cause list
Ice blockage considered given weather data
Output is plain English with no technical jargon

checkpoints.description: Intermediate done states the agent should report at. Maps to the CHECKPOINT pattern used in Clode's briefings.

description.description: What the agent should have completed and reported at this checkpoint.

blocking.description: If true, agent must pause and await confirmation before proceeding past this checkpoint.

output_format.description: Expected format of the agent's output.

output_destination.description: Where the output should go. File path, repo, or null for inline response.

review.description: Quality gate. Who checks the output and what happens if it fails.

reviewer.description: Agent ID of the reviewer. Jean-Claude by default. Joan Claude for technical output. Clyde for plans and sequencing.

review_criteria.description: What the reviewer is checking for. If omitted, reviewer uses acceptance_criteria.

on_failure.description: What happens if the output fails review. retry_different_vibe spawns the same responsibility with a different vibe.

max_retries.description: Maximum retry attempts before escalating or failing.

response.description: The response contract. Defines the structure the receiving agent must use when reporting back.

format.description: are_response means the agent returns a structured ARE response envelope. Other formats are for lightweight or human-readable responses.

return_to.description: Agent ID to return the response to. Defaults to the from.agent_id sender.

include_reasoning.description: Whether the agent should include its reasoning chain in the response, not just the output.

include_checkpoint_log.description: Whether completed checkpoint states should be included in the response.

metadata.description: Optional envelope metadata for observability, debugging, and audit.

tags.description: Arbitrary tags for filtering and grouping envelopes.

notes.description: Human-readable notes about this envelope. Not read by agents.
